claim_chain: An infaunal crisis marked by disappearance of pre-extinction ichnofauna is identified in late Changhsingian–early Smithian successions
Early recovery ichnofauna in late Smithian was short-lived and characterized by dwarfism and monospecific assemblages
Abundant trace fossils in Spathian HSG Formation comprise 16 ichnogenera representing morphologically complex ichnofauna
Large burrows in Spathian indicate occurrence of advanced ecosystem engineers
Infaunalization was a vital strategy for invertebrates to survive and thrive under harsh post-extinction conditions
Diverse community of HSG Formation suggests rapid recovery of life in low-latitude terrestrial environments as early as ~2 Myr after end-Permian crisis

typical_pattern: State observation or finding with temporal/spatial context
Provide detailed description of fossil assemblages with quantitative data
Compare with pre-extinction or earlier recovery stages
Connect to broader implications for recovery timing or survival strategies

likely_priorities: Temporal resolution and geochronological constraints on recovery timing
Quantitative support for ichnological diversity and bioturbation intensity claims
Biological identification and ecological interpretation of trace makers
Comparison with marine recovery patterns and other terrestrial localities
Environmental context and climatic conditions during recovery phases
likely_attacks: Uncertainty in biological nature of large burrows despite poor preservation
Limited specimens hindering definite taxonomic designation
Inference of predation pressure or competition from trace fossil patterns alone
Generalization from North China locality to broader low-latitude recovery patterns
Causal links between burrowing behavior and specific environmental stressors
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly acknowledges uncertainty in several places: 'poor preservation and limited specimens hinder a definite designation' for large burrows; 'the biological nature of these large burrows cannot be confirmed'; uses cautious language like 'may indicate' for adaptive responses; 'we envisage that infaunalization could also have been a vital strategy' shows hedging on mechanistic interpretations. The paper also carefully qualifies temporal constraints with 'about late Smithian in age' and uses 'probably produced by' for trace maker identification, suggesting awareness that reviewers would scrutinize taxonomic and temporal precision.

examples.quoted_phrase: An infaunal crisis, marked by the disappearance of the moderately diversified pre-extinction ichnofauna and the absence of biogenic structures, is identified
we envisage that infaunalization could also have been a vital strategy for invertebrates to survive and thrive
suggests a rapid recovery of life in low-latitude terrestrial environments in the Spathian, as early as ~2 Myr after the end-Permian biotic crisis
may indicate an adaptive response to heightened predation pressure or competition for available resources
The infaunal crisis was contemporary with the extirpation of the pareiasaur fauna

phrase_evidence.phrase: An infaunal crisis, marked by the disappearance of the moderately diversified pre-extinction ichnofauna
The greatest mass extinction at the end of the Permian, ca. 252 million years ago, led to a tropical dead zone
Here, we show fast reestablishment of riparian ecosystems
as little as ~2 million years after the end-Permian mass extinction
Both ichnodiversity and ichnodisparity decline abruptly
Dwarfism in trace makers is observed from all ichnogenera
with burrow sizes reduced from a mean of 4.06 mm before the EPME (n=779)–2.06 mm (n=341) in the late Smithian
The depauperate ichnofauna of the late Smithian was monospecific
representing initial recolonization of empty niches by opportunists
However, recurrent occurrences of microbially induced sedimentary structures (MISS) in the Liujiagou Formation show that depressed ecosystems persisted
Abundant trace fossils are identified in the Spathian HSG Formation, comprising 16 ichnogenera of nine ichnomorphs
This morphologically complex ichnofauna was dominated by actively filled burrows
resulting in moderately to substantially bioturbated deposits, with ii 2–3 and BI 3–4 at most layers
average burrow sizes of all ichnogenera also increased to 3.9 mm in the HSG (n=2241
Several large burrows in the Spathian indicate the occurrence of advanced ecosystem engineers
Although the biological nature of these large burrows cannot be confirmed
their activities increased biogenic reworking of sediment and soils, improved geochemical recycling and ecosystem complexity
implying key roles in ecosystem functioning
we envisage that infaunalization could also have been a vital strategy
may indicate an adaptive response to heightened predation pressure or competition for available resources
Our results also shed light on the timing of the TDZ
which constrain persistence of the TDZ to the late Smithian in North China
suggests a rapid recovery of life in low-latitude terrestrial environments in the Spathian
enhanced burrowing behavior is considered a key survival-recovery strategy
suggest that the riverain regions could be refugia for the reorganization of post-EPME ecosystem
Shallow tiers Kouphichnium and Helminthoidichnites (He) are crosscut by immediate tier Palaeophycus (Pa)
Skull elements of Xilousuchus sapingensis and drawings
poor preservation and limited specimens hinder a definite designation

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. Quantitative values (4.06 mm, 2.06 mm, 3.9 mm, n=779, n=341, n=2241, ~2 Myr, 252 Ma) appear verbatim in the input. No external references, author names, or invented data were included. The profile accurately reflects the paper's paleontological focus, temporal progression from crisis through recovery, and balanced claim strength with appropriate hedging.
